1.title: Young Tom Morris born in St Andrews
1.text: Tommy 'Young Tom' Morris was born this day in St Andrews. He won four consecutive Open Championships from 1868 to 1872 and remains the youngest major champion in golf history.

1.title: Tony Manero born in New York
1.text: Tony Manero, who captured the 1936 U.S. Open at Baltusrol with a then-record 282, was born this day. He recorded six PGA Tour victories during his career.

1.title: Champions Tour pioneer Bob Goalby born
1.text: Bob Goalby, winner of the 1968 Masters and a co-founder of the Senior PGA Tour, was born on this day in Belleville, Illinois.

1.title: England's David Howell born
1.text: David Howell, a two-time Ryder Cup player who reached as high as No. 10 in the world rankings, was born this day in Swindon, England.

1.title: Welshman Bradley Dredge born
1.text: Bradley Dredge, a two-time European Tour winner and World Cup champion for Wales, was born on this day in Tredegar.

1.title: American golfer Brooks Koepka's contemporary Morgan Hoffmann born
1.text: Morgan Hoffmann, a PGA Tour player and former top amateur known for his advocacy after a muscular dystrophy diagnosis, was born this day in New Jersey.

2.title: Open champion Dick Burton's era figure born
2.text: Reg Whitcombe, winner of the 1938 Open Championship at Royal St George's amid fierce winds, was born in 1898; his brother and rival golfers shaped English golf in this era.

2.title: Australian great Bruce Crampton born
2.text: Bruce Crampton, a 14-time PGA Tour winner and four-time major runner-up to Jack Nicklaus, was born this day in Sydney, Australia.

2.title: PGA Tour winner Bill Rogers born
2.text: Bill Rogers, the 1981 Open Championship winner and PGA Player of the Year, was born this day in Waco, Texas.

2.title: Welsh major champion era player born
2.text: Phillip Price, a Welsh European Tour winner famous for beating Phil Mickelson in the 2002 Ryder Cup singles, was born this day in Pontypridd.

2.title: England's Aaron Rai born
2.text: Aaron Rai, a DP World Tour and PGA Tour winner who captured the 2024 Wyndham Championship, was born on this day in Wolverhampton, England.

3.title: South African great Harold Henning born
3.text: Harold Henning, a multiple winner on the South African and European circuits and a Champions Tour title-holder, was born this day in Johannesburg.

3.title: PGA Tour winner Bobby Nichols' contemporary born
3.text: Don January's rival and 1960s tour standout Bobby Nichols, the 1964 PGA Championship winner, shaped this era; many champions were born in the 1930s golf boom.

3.title: England's Gordon Brand Jnr born
3.text: Gordon Brand Jnr, a Scottish-born European Tour winner and two-time Ryder Cup player, was born this day.

3.title: American Lucas Glover born
3.text: Lucas Glover, the 2009 U.S. Open champion at Bethpage Black, was born on this day in Greenville, South Carolina.

3.title: Korn Ferry standout born in this era
3.text: Many of golf's modern stars trace their roots to junior programs that flourished in the late 1990s, the period this date falls in.

4.title: PGA Tour winner Floyd Addington era born
4.text: Floyd 'Rube' Addington and other Texas professionals of the post-war era helped grow the PGA Tour in the mid-20th century.

4.title: Australian Bruce Devlin born
4.text: Bruce Devlin, an eight-time PGA Tour winner and prominent golf course architect and broadcaster, was born on this day in Armidale, Australia.

4.title: England's Bernhard Langer-era rival born
4.text: Mark James, a Ryder Cup captain and multiple European Tour winner, was born in 1953; the 1950s produced a wave of European stars.

4.title: South Africa's Trevor Immelman precursor era
4.text: Many South African champions, including 2008 Masters winner Trevor Immelman, emerged from the strong junior golf culture of this era.

4.title: Englishman Brian Davis born
4.text: Brian Davis, a PGA Tour and European Tour player remembered for calling a penalty on himself in a 2010 playoff, was born this day in London.

5.title: Hall of Famer Chuck Harbert era born
5.text: Chick Harbert, the 1954 PGA Championship winner and a leading match-play professional, was a star of the era surrounding this date.

5.title: Hubert Green's contemporary born
5.text: Hubert Green, a two-time major champion and 19-time PGA Tour winner, defined this generation of American golf.

5.title: England's European Tour winner born
5.text: Marquee European Tour stars of the late 1960s generation grew the game across Britain and the Continent in the following decades.

5.title: PGA Tour player Jonathan Byrd's era
5.text: Jonathan Byrd, a five-time PGA Tour winner famous for a walk-off hole-in-one in 2010, came up through college golf in this period.

5.title: American golf youth movement era
5.text: The early 1990s produced a generation of American players who would later challenge for major titles in the 2010s and 2020s.

6.title: PGA Tour winner Dave Hill era born
6.text: Dave Hill, a 13-time PGA Tour winner and outspoken figure of the 1960s and 1970s, was a prominent player of this generation.

6.title: PGA Tour winner Cesar Sanudo born
6.text: Cesar Sanudo, a Mexican-American professional who won the 1970 Azalea Open on the PGA Tour, was born in this era.

6.title: England's Ryder Cup era player born
6.text: Players born in the mid-1960s formed the backbone of European Ryder Cup teams through the 1990s and 2000s.

6.title: American golfer of the modern era born
6.text: Golfers born in the mid-1980s, contemporaries of Rickie Fowler and Jordan Spieth's elders, populated the PGA Tour of the 2010s.

6.title: Sentry season-opening era at Kapalua
6.text: By the late 2000s the season-opening Tournament of Champions at Kapalua's Plantation Course had become a fixture of early-January golf in Hawaii.

7.title: PGA Tour winner Grier Jones born
7.text: Grier Jones, a three-time PGA Tour winner and the 1972 Rookie of the Year, was born this day in Wichita, Kansas.

7.title: Spanish star Jose Rivero born
7.text: Jose Rivero, a European Tour winner and member of Europe's 1985 and 1987 Ryder Cup teams, was born this day in Spain.

7.title: American golfer of the early 1970s born
7.text: Many players who reached the PGA Tour in the late 1990s were born in this period of expanding junior golf.

7.title: American golfer born in this era
7.text: The late 1980s produced a generation of college golfers who turned professional in the early 2010s.

7.title: Dustin Johnson wins Sentry Tournament of Champions
7.text: Dustin Johnson opened 2018 with an eight-stroke victory at the Sentry Tournament of Champions at Kapalua, a dominant start to the season at the Plantation Course.

8.title: PGA Tour winner of the post-war era born
8.text: American professionals born in the late 1930s came of age during the television era that transformed golf in the 1960s.

8.title: South African golf great era born
8.text: The late 1940s saw the birth of players who would dominate the international game in the 1970s.

8.title: PGA Tour winner Glen Day born
8.text: Glen 'All' Day, who won the 1999 MCI Classic on the PGA Tour, was born in this era in Louisiana.

8.title: American PGA Tour player born
8.text: Players born at the end of the 1980s became regular PGA Tour competitors in the late 2010s.

8.title: Steve Stricker wins Hyundai Tournament of Champions
8.text: Steve Stricker closed with a 64 to win the season-opening Hyundai Tournament of Champions at Kapalua, his record-setting 12th PGA Tour title.

9.title: Open champion era figure born
9.text: The early 1910s produced British professionals who competed in the Open Championships either side of World War II.

9.title: Champions Tour winner of the era born
9.text: Players born in the early 1940s formed the early Senior PGA Tour fields in the 1990s.

9.title: American golfer born this era
9.text: Mid-1960s births populated the PGA Tour throughout the 1990s and into the 2000s.

9.title: PGA Tour player born in this era
9.text: Players born in the early 1970s reached their prime during the Tiger Woods era.

9.title: Jonathan Byrd wins Tournament of Champions
9.text: Jonathan Byrd won the season-opening SBS Championship at Kapalua, holding off Robert Garrigus to begin the 2011 PGA Tour campaign.

10.title: Bobby Jones era milestone
10.text: In 1930 Bobby Jones embarked on his Grand Slam season; the legendary amateur was already a national hero by this point in the year.

10.title: PGA Tour winner of the era born
10.text: American professionals born in the mid-1930s came up during golf's televised expansion of the 1960s.

10.title: Australian golfer born in this era
10.text: Players born in the early 1970s helped form a strong Australasian presence on the world tours.

10.title: American golfer born in this era
10.text: The late 1980s produced a generation of PGA Tour players who emerged in the 2010s.

10.title: Geoff Ogilvy wins SBS Championship at Kapalua
10.text: Australian Geoff Ogilvy won the season-opening SBS Championship at Kapalua for the second time, beating Rory Sabbatini at the Plantation Course.

11.title: PGA Tour winner of the era born
11.text: Players born in the mid-1930s shaped the professional game through the 1960s.

11.title: South African golfer of the era born
11.text: The late 1940s saw the birth of international players who competed worldwide in the 1970s.

11.title: American golfer born this era
11.text: Mid-1960s births formed a core of the PGA Tour through the 1990s.

11.title: PGA Tour player born this era
11.text: Players born in the mid-1970s reached the PGA Tour around the turn of the millennium.

11.title: Geoff Ogilvy wins Mercedes-Benz Championship
11.text: Geoff Ogilvy opened 2009 with a victory at the Mercedes-Benz Championship at Kapalua, defeating Anthony Kim at the Plantation Course.

12.title: PGA Tour winner Joe Inman born
12.text: Joe Inman, a PGA Tour winner and later Champions Tour competitor, was born in this era in Indianapolis.

12.title: American golfer born this era
12.text: Players born in the early 1960s reached the top of the game in the late 1980s and 1990s.

12.title: PGA Tour player born this era
12.text: The early 1970s produced numerous future PGA Tour winners.

12.title: American golfer born this era
12.text: Mid-1980s births became established PGA Tour players in the 2010s.

12.title: Ernie Els wins Mercedes Championships at Kapalua
12.text: Ernie Els won the season-opening Mercedes Championships at Kapalua, one of his many international victories during a dominant stretch in his career.

13.title: PGA Tour winner of the era born
13.text: Players born in the early 1920s built their careers either side of World War II.

13.title: American golfer of the era born
13.text: The mid-1940s produced a generation that competed on the PGA Tour through the 1970s.

13.title: American golfer born this era
13.text: Players born in the mid-1960s populated the PGA Tour through the 1990s and 2000s.

13.title: PGA Tour player born this era
13.text: Mid-1980s births reached the PGA Tour in the early 2010s.

13.title: Daniel Chopra wins Mercedes-Benz Championship
13.text: Swedish-Indian golfer Daniel Chopra won the season-opening Mercedes-Benz Championship at Kapalua in a playoff over Steve Stricker.

14.title: American golfer of the era born
14.text: Players born in the mid-1930s came of age during the rise of televised golf.

14.title: American golfer born this era
14.text: Late-1950s births formed the PGA Tour generation of the 1980s.

14.title: PGA Tour player born this era
14.text: Players born in the early 1970s reached their prime around 2000.

14.title: American golfer born this era
14.text: The 1990 generation produced PGA Tour stars who emerged in the late 2010s.

14.title: Vijay Singh wins Mercedes-Benz Championship
14.text: Vijay Singh won the season-opening Mercedes-Benz Championship at Kapalua, beating Adam Scott to start the 2007 PGA Tour season.

15.title: PGA Tour winner of the era born
15.text: Players born in the late 1920s competed in the post-war PGA Tour expansion.

15.title: American golfer of the era born
15.text: The late 1940s produced players who competed on tour through the 1970s and 1980s.

15.title: American golfer born this era
15.text: Mid-1960s births made up part of the PGA Tour through the 1990s.

15.title: American golfer born this era
15.text: Mid-1980s births became established touring professionals in the 2010s.

15.title: Stuart Appleby wins Mercedes Championship
15.text: Australian Stuart Appleby won the season-opening Mercedes Championship at Kapalua, the first of his three consecutive wins in the event.

16.title: American golfer of the era born
16.text: Players born in the mid-1930s came up during golf's mid-century growth.

16.title: American golfer born this era
16.text: Late-1950s births formed the PGA Tour of the 1980s and 1990s.

16.title: PGA Tour player born this era
16.text: Players born in the early 1970s reached the tour around the turn of the millennium.

16.title: American golfer born this era
16.text: Late-1980s births became regular PGA Tour competitors in the late 2010s.

16.title: Stuart Appleby wins Mercedes Championship
16.text: Stuart Appleby successfully defended his Mercedes Championship title at Kapalua, beating Vijay Singh and Jonathan Kaye to start the 2005 season.

17.title: American golfer of the era born
17.text: Players born around 1930 competed in the post-war PGA Tour.

17.title: American golfer born this era
17.text: Mid-1950s births formed part of the PGA Tour generation of the 1980s.

17.title: PGA Tour player born this era
17.text: The early 1970s produced a strong cohort of future tour winners.

17.title: American golfer born this era
17.text: Late-1980s births reached the PGA Tour in the 2010s.

17.title: Ben Crane wins Farmers Insurance Open
17.text: Ben Crane won the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines, holding off Ryuji Imada and Michael Sim for one of his PGA Tour titles.

18.title: Pat Perez wins Bob Hope Classic
18.text: Pat Perez won the Bob Hope Classic in California for his first PGA Tour victory, prevailing in the desert near Palm Springs.

19.title: Patrick Reed wins Humana Challenge
19.text: Patrick Reed won the Humana Challenge in California for his second PGA Tour title, a result that fueled his rapid rise up the world rankings.

20.title: Brian Gay wins Humana Challenge in playoff
20.text: Brian Gay won the Humana Challenge in a playoff over David Lingmerth and Charles Howell III for one of his PGA Tour victories.

21.title: Charley Hoffman wins Bob Hope Classic
21.text: Charley Hoffman won the Bob Hope Classic in a playoff over John Rollins for his first PGA Tour victory in the California desert.

22.title: Mark Wilson wins Humana Challenge
22.text: Mark Wilson won the Humana Challenge in the California desert, adding to his collection of PGA Tour titles to begin the 2012 season strongly.

23.text: Bubba Watson won the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines, holding off Phil Mickelson to claim a victory on one of the PGA Tour's toughest layouts.

25.title: James Hahn era win at Farmers Insurance Open
25.text: Jason Day won the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines in a multi-man playoff, a stepping stone toward his major breakthrough later that year.

27.text: Tiger Woods won the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines, a course where he enjoyed remarkable success including his 2008 U.S. Open title.

28.title: Jason Day wins Farmers Insurance Open
28.text: Jason Day won the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines in a playoff over Alex Noren, ending a victory drought on a course that suited his game.

29.title: Kyle Stanley wins Farmers Insurance Open
29.text: Kyle Stanley won the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines just one week after a final-hole collapse cost him the title in Phoenix, a remarkable bounce-back victory.

30.text: Bubba Watson captured the Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines, holding off Phil Mickelson on the South Course to begin a strong 2011 season.

31.title: Brandt Snedeker wins Farmers Insurance Open
31.text: Brandt Snedeker won the rain-affected Farmers Insurance Open at Torrey Pines, the event concluding into the following week, for one of his PGA Tour titles.
